channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Adani Group Denies Airline Expansion Plans Amid Industry Challenges
item.description: The Adani Group has categorically denied any plans to enter the airline business, countering recent media reports and market speculation. A spokesperson for Adani Enterprises Limited stated that the r...

item.title: Jennifer Lopez's High School Years: From Athlete to Global Superstar
item.description: Before becoming a global superstar, Jennifer Lopez attended Preston High School, an all-girls private Catholic school in the Bronx, graduating in 1987. During her high school years, she balanced acade...

item.title: Kim Kardashian and Kanye West's legal dispute over child custody and financial matters
item.description: Kim Kardashian and Kanye West are embroiled in a high-profile legal battle over child custody, financial responsibilities, and the division of assets from their marriage. The case, which has drawn sig...

item.title: Pakistan Army Chief Promoted to National Strategic Command Role
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has promoted Lieutenant General Aamer Raza, the chief of general staff of the Pakistan Army, to the rank of four-star general and appointed him as Commander National Stra...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Clashes Escalate Over Control of Strategic Shipping Routes
item.description: The U.S. military has intensified strikes against Iran amid escalating tensions over control of critical shipping routes, particularly the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el Mandeb. These strategic water...

item.title: Nairobi Woman's Balcony Escape Due to Landlord Rent Dispute
item.description: A Nairobi woman named Joy has gained attention for her unique morning routine of escaping her apartment via a balcony after her landlord locked the building entrance due to unpaid rent. The incident, ...

item.title: DA alleges political bias and mismanagement in McKenzie's appointments and FIFA spending
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has formally requested the public protector to investigate Sport, Arts and Culture Minister Gayton McKenzie over allegations of breaching the Executive Ethics Code. The co...

item.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Conditional on Recognizing Israel
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel has created uncertainty around the agreement, which was signed just days prio...

item.title: Tanzania Leads Kenya in Tourism Revenue Growth
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania's dominance in tourism earnings compared to Kenya, marking a significant economic achievement. It notes that Tanzania's tourism sector has outperformed Kenya's, contrib...

item.title: PFN Calls on Tinubu to Reject Anti-Preaching Bill Amid Concerns Over Religious Freedom
item.description: The Pentecostal Fellowship of Nigeria (PFN) has strongly urged President Bola Tinubu to reject the proposed anti-preaching and hawking bill, calling it unnecessary and targeted at Christians. The Chri...

item.title: Afreximbank funds Shoreline's Algerian oilfield project
item.description: Afreximbank has approved a $200 million loan to support Shoreline Power Company and its Italian subsidiary Arkad S.p.A. in executing a major oilfield project in Algeria. This financing will enable the...

item.title: PM Appoints Gen Aamer Raza as National Strategic Command Commander
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has appointed General Aamer Raza as the Commander of the National Strategic Command, promoting him from Lieutenant General to Four-Star General. This appointment comes un...

item.title: Alexa and Gelo Continue Their Chemistry in 'The Chambermaid's Daughter' Season 2
item.description: The Chambermaid's Daughter Season 2, starring Alexa Ilacad and Gelo Rivera, is set to captivate audiences with its compelling storyline and the actors' enduring chemistry. The series, produced by ABS-...

item.title: Sault Man Arrested Twice in 12 Hours for Same Victim
item.description: A man from Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, was arrested twice within a 12-hour period for allegedly targeting the same victim. The incidents occurred on July 23, 2026, with the first arrest made at 8:08 PM...

item.title: Johnson Apologizes for Mischaracterizing Mokwele's Qualifications in SAPS Post Dispute
item.description: Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) head Andrea Johnson has retracted her claim that Brig Dineo Mokwele was unqualified for a senior SAPS post after reviewing her files. The admission ...

item.title: Pauline Hanson Faces Criticism for Downplaying Domestic Violence Comments
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has faced intense criticism from domestic violence experts and advocates after her comments about domestic violence being a 'two-way street' were widely condemned. Han...

item.title: Senators Criticize Duale's Tribal Remarks, Demand Formal Senate Motion
item.description: Three Kenyan senators have criticized Health Cabinet Secretary Aden Duale over alleged remarks in a viral video that they claim risk inflaming ethnic tensions ahead of elections. The senators, includi...

item.title: JAMB and Borno State Collaborate on Mega CBT Centre for Education
item.description: Ishaq Oloyede, JAMB Registrar, commissioned a state-of-the-art CBT centre in Borno, funded by the state government. The facility, built on 5,300sqm, has 275 computers, 16 convenience facilities, and 5...

item.title: eThekwini Municipality Faces Water Crisis Amid Contractor Disputes and Service Disruptions
item.description: The eThekwini municipality is grappling with severe water service challenges, exacerbated by disputes between contractors and municipal officials. Contractors blocked access to a water services depot ...

item.title: Kilifi College Set to Start Classes for 1,000 Students in September
item.description: Ronald Ngala Utalii College in Kilifi is finally ready to start admitting students after a 10-year wait. The college, which focuses on tourism-related courses like catering, hotel management, and seaf...
